summary: Descriptive overview of quasicontract in substantive law: unjust enrichment, quantum meruit, no actual agreement.
key_points: unjust enrichment is a fundamental concept in contract law.
Legal standards for quasicontract are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine quasicontract in litigation.
The doctrine of quasicontract has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding quasicontract requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
